dialogues: Think about where you started. You found equal groups in bags and circles. You learned that multiplication means groups of the same size. You built expressions, then equations. You discovered arrays: organized groups with rows and columns. And today, you proved something powerful: you can multiply in any order and always get the same answer.
You didn't just memorize that. You proved it with highlighting and rotation. You used it as a strategy. That's real mathematical thinking.
